original_abstract: Data visualization is a cornerstone of scientific communication, yet many researchers are hindered by the steep learning curves of programming-based tools or the high licensing costs of commercial software. To bridge this gap, we developed SRplot (Scientific and Research plot tool), a comprehensive, free online platform designed for intuitive data visualization and graphing. SRplot integrates over 120 specialized modules tailored for biomedical and bioinformatics research, including tools for genomics, transcriptomics, clinical data, and network analysis. The platform features a user-friendly graphical interface that allows researchers to generate high-quality plots in real-time by simply pasting their datasets. Key advantages include server-side computation, which eliminates the need for high-performance local hardware, and the inclusion of built-in biological databases such as GO, KEGG, and TCGA. Users can extensively customize graphical parameters and export publication-ready figures in both bitmap (PNG, TIFF) and vector (PDF, SVG) formats. Since its inception, SRplot has supported more than 46,000 users and has been cited in over 550 peer-reviewed journals. SRplot is continuously updated based on community feedback and is freely accessible at http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot.
humanized_abstract: Data visualization is an indispensable part of scientific communication, but writing code for specialized plotting programs such as R, Python, and genoplot can take a long time, and the price of commercial graphing software is too expensive for many scientists. Recently, we developed a new scientific and research plot tool SRplot (SRplot for short) that implements advanced data visualization and graphing functions through an easy-to-use web interface. To date, SRplot has implemented 120+ functional modules that are applicable to various fields of biomedical and bioinformatics research such as genomics, transcriptomics, clinical data and network analysis. All that scientists need to do is to copy and paste their data into the input interface of SRplot and then generate their desired plots quickly. All computational work is performed on the SRplot server, so there is no need for local hardware of any type. In addition, the GO, KEGG and TCGA databases are incorporated into SRplot. Many customizable options are available in SRplot, and all kinds of publication plots such as PNG, TIFF, PDF and SVG can be easily be generated from SRplot. So far, SRplot has served 46,000+ users and has been cited in over 550 papers in prestigious journals. SRplot is a dynamic tool, which is developed under the guidelines of user feedback, and is free available at http://www.bioinformatics.com.cn/SRplot.
